lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਅੰਧੈ ਊਂਧੈ ਕਵਲਿ ਦਿਸਨੑਿ ਖਰੇ ਕਰੂਪ ॥
lines.translit: man andhai aoondhai kaval disana khare karoop |
lines.english: Their minds are blind, and their heart-lotuses are upside-down; they look totally ugly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਹੁ ਜਿ ਅੰਧੇ ਕੂਪ ਕਹਿਆ ਬਿਰਦੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੑੀ ॥
lines.translit: manahu ji andhe koop kahiaa birad na jaananaee |
lines.english: Those mortals whose minds are like deep dark pits do not understand the purpose of life, even when it is explained to them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਨਾਦ ਨ ਬੇਦ ਨ ਗੀਅ ਰਸੁ ਰਸ ਕਸ ਨ ਜਾਣੰਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa naad na bed na geea ras ras kas na jaanant |
lines.english: Some do not understand about the Sound-current of the Naad or the Vedas, music, virtue or vice.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਿ ਕਹਿ ਜਾਣਹਿ ਕਹਿਆ ਬੁਝਹਿ ਤੇ ਨਰ ਸੁਘੜ ਸਰੂਪ ॥
lines.translit: eik keh jaaneh kahiaa bujheh te nar sugharr saroop |
lines.english: Some know how to speak, and understand what they are told. They are wise and beautiful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਸੁਧਿ ਨ ਬੁਧਿ ਨ ਅਕਲਿ ਸਰ ਅਖਰ ਕਾ ਭੇਉ ਨ ਲਹੰਤਿ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa sudh na budh na akal sar akhar kaa bheo na lahant |
lines.english: Some are not blessed with understanding, intelligence, or sublime intellect; they do not grasp the mystery of God's Word.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੇ ਨਰ ਅਸਲਿ ਖਰ ਜਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਣ ਗਰਬੁ ਕਰੰਤਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak se nar asal khar ji bin gun garab karant |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, they are donkeys; they are very proud of themselves, but they have no virtues at all. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਅਰਥਿ ਜੋ ਖਰਚਦੇ ਦੇਂਦੇ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: har arath jo kharachade dende sukh paaeaa |
lines.english: Those who spend the wealth of the Lord find peace through giving.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਭ ਪਵਿਤੁ ਹੈ ਧਨੁ ਸੰਪੈ ਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sabh pavit hai dhan sanpai maaeaa |
lines.english: To the Gurmukh, everything is sacred: wealth, property, Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਤਿਨ ਤੋਟਿ ਨ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jo har naam dhiaaeide tin tott na aaeaa |
lines.english: Those who meditate on the Lord's Name shall never be deprived.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਾਂ ਨਦਰੀ ਆਵਦਾ ਮਾਇਆ ਸੁਟਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukhaan nadaree aavadaa maaeaa sutt paaeaa |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs come to see the Lord, and leave behind the things of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਭਗਤਾਂ ਹੋਰੁ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ ॥੨੨॥
lines.translit: naanak bhagataan hor chit na aavee har naam samaaeaa |22|
lines.english: O Nanak, the devotees do not think of anything else; they are absorbed in the Name of the Lord. ||22||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Salok, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਜਿਨੑਾ ਏਕ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: sachai sabad jinaa ek liv laagee |
lines.english: They are lovingly attuned to the True Shabad, the Word of the One God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਰਹ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਮਹਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਧੀ ॥
lines.translit: girah kuttanb meh sehaj samaadhee |
lines.english: In their own household and family, they are in natural Samaadhi.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਨਿ ਸੇ ਵਡਭਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: satigur sevan se vaddabhaagee |
lines.english: Those who serve the True Guru are very fortunate.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਣਤੈ ਸੇਵ ਨ ਹੋਵਈ ਕੀਤਾ ਥਾਇ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ganatai sev na hovee keetaa thaae na paae |
lines.english: Calculated service is not service at all, and what is done is not approved.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦੈ ਸਾਦੁ ਨ ਆਇਓ ਸਚਿ ਨ ਲਗੋ ਭਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sabadai saad na aaeo sach na lago bhaau |
lines.english: The flavor of the Shabad, the Word of God, is not tasted if the mortal is not in love with the True Lord God.

lines.translit: mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਸੇ ਸਚੇ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate se sache bairaagee |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are attuned to the Naam are truly detached from the world. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਪਿਆਰਾ ਨ ਲਗਈ ਮਨਹਠਿ ਆਵੈ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur piaaraa na lagee manahatth aavai jaae |
lines.english: The stubborn-minded person does not even like the True Guru; he comes and goes in reincarnation.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਇਕ ਵਿਖ ਅਗਾਹਾ ਭਰੇ ਤਾਂ ਦਸ ਵਿਖਾਂ ਪਿਛਾਹਾ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je ik vikh agaahaa bhare taan das vikhaan pichhaahaa jaae |
lines.english: He takes one step forward, and ten steps back.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਚਾਕਰੀ ਜੇ ਚਲਹਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਭਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur kee sevaa chaakaree je chaleh satigur bhaae |
lines.english: The mortal works to serve the True Guru, if he walks in harmony with the True Guru's Will.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਨੋ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਹਜੇ ਰਹੈ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: aap gavaae satiguroo no milai sahaje rahai samaae |
lines.english: He loses his self-conceit, and meets the True Guru; he remains intuitively absorbed in the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਨੑਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਸਚੇ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak tinaa naam na veesarai sache mel milaae |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, they never forget the Naam, the Name of the Lord; they are united in Union with the True Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਇਨ ਸਾਖਤਿ ਪਉਣ ਵੇਗ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਚਤੁਰਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: soein saakhat paun veg dhrig dhrig chaturaaee |
lines.english: Their gold and horses, fast as the wind, are cursed, and cursed are their clever tricks.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਨ ਮਲੂਕ ਕਹਾਇਦੇ ਕੋ ਰਹਣੁ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: khaan malook kahaaeide ko rehan na paaee |
lines.english: They call themselves emperors and rulers, but none of them will be allowed to stay.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੜੑ ਮੰਦਰ ਗਚ ਗੀਰੀਆ ਕਿਛੁ ਸਾਥਿ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: garra mandar gach geereea kichh saath na jaaee |
lines.english: Their sturdy forts and mansions - none of them will go along with them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੋ ਦੇਵੈ ਤਿਸਹਿ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੑੀ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਪਾਈ ॥੨੩॥
lines.translit: naanak jo devai tiseh na jaananaee manamukh dukh paaee |23|
lines.english: O Nanak, the self-willed manmukh does not know the One who gives, and so he suffers in pain. ||23||

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਤੀਹ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪਰਕਾਰ ਕਰਹਿ ਬਹੁ ਮੈਲੁ ਵਧਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: chhateeh amrit parakaar kareh bahu mail vadhaaee |
lines.english: Eating the thirty-six delicacies, they become bloated with pollution.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੜੑਿ ਪੜੑਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਮੋੁਨੀ ਥਕੇ ਦੇਸੰਤਰ ਭਵਿ ਥਕੇ ਭੇਖਧਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: parra parra panddit mounee thake desantar bhav thake bhekhadhaaree |
lines.english: The Pandits, the religious scholars and the silent sages read and recite until they get tired. They wander through foreign lands in their religious robes, until they are exhausted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਭਾਇ ਨਾਉ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਪਾਇਨਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਲਾਗਾ ਅਤਿ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: doojai bhaae naau kade na paaein dukh laagaa at bhaaree |
lines.english: In love with duality, they never receive the Name. Held in the grasp of pain, they suffer terribly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੂਰਖ ਅੰਧੇ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਕੈ ਬਿਉਹਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: moorakh andhe trai gun seveh maaeaa kai biauhaaree |
lines.english: The blind fools serve the three gunas, the three dispositions; they deal only with Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਕਪਟੁ ਉਦਰੁ ਭਰਣ ਕੈ ਤਾਈ ਪਾਠ ਪੜਹਿ ਗਾਵਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: andar kapatt udar bharan kai taaee paatth parreh gaavaaree |
lines.english: With deception in their hearts, the fools read sacred texts to fill their bellies.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵੇ ਸੋ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਏ ਜਿਨ ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਮਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: satigur seve so sukh paae jin haumai vichahu maaree |
lines.english: One who serves the True Guru finds peace; he eradicates egotism from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪੜਣਾ ਗੁਨਣਾ ਇਕੁ ਨਾਉ ਹੈ ਬੂਝੈ ਕੋ ਬੀਚਾਰੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak parranaa gunanaa ik naau hai boojhai ko beechaaree |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, there is One Name to chant and dwell on; how rare are those who reflect on this and understand. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਂਗੇ ਆਵਣਾ ਨਾਂਗੇ ਜਾਣਾ ਹਰਿ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਕਿਆ ਕੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: naange aavanaa naange jaanaa har hukam paaeaa kiaa keejai |
lines.english: Naked we come, and naked we go. This is by the Lord's Command; what else can we do?

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਕੀ ਵਸਤੁ ਸੋਈ ਲੈ ਜਾਇਗਾ ਰੋਸੁ ਕਿਸੈ ਸਿਉ ਕੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: jis kee vasat soee lai jaaeigaa ros kisai siau keejai |
lines.english: The object belongs to Him; He shall take it away; with whom should one be angry.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਸਦਾ ਸਲਾਹਿਹੁ ਰਸਨਾ ਰਾਮੁ ਰਵੀਜੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak sukhadaataa sadaa salaahihu rasanaa raam raveejai |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, praise the Giver of peace forever; with your tongue, savor the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਸੁ ਭਾਣਾ ਮੰਨੇ ਸਹਜੇ ਹਰਿ ਰਸੁ ਪੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh hovai su bhaanaa mane sahaje har ras peejai |
lines.english: One who becomes Gurmukh accepts God's Will; he intuitively drinks in the Lord's sublime essence.
